module.name: Search
module.param: | rest /services/apps/local splunk_server=local | search title="cisco_cdr" | stats count last(title) as appContext | fillnull appContext value="$sideview.currentApp$"

module.name: Search
module.param: | rest /servicesNS/-/-/configs/conf-app splunk_server=local | rename eai:acl.app as app | search app="$sideview.currentApp$" | stats last(build) as build last(version) as version last(description) as description | appendcols [ | rest /services/apps/local splunk_server=local | search title="cisco_cdr" | stats count | eval installation_state=if("$test_cisco_cdr_not_installed$_foo"=="1_foo" OR count=0,"cisco_cdr_not_installed","cisco_cdr_is_installed") | table installation_state ] | appendcols [ | inputlookup data_types | search app="$sideview.currentApp$" | stats count | eval data_type_enabled=if(count==0,0,1) ] | eval installation_state=if(installation_state=="cisco_cdr_is_installed" AND data_type_enabled==0,"cisco_cdr_installed_but_data_table_not_enabled", installation_state)

module.name: HTML
module.param:
How do we use this app?

Start with the documentation for this app if you haven't already.

This app is designed to be used in conjunction with our "TA_expressway_cdr" app, which you deploy to your indexing tier. This app, which you have correctly installed on the Search Head tier (nicely done), does all of the search-time field extraction, and it also implements some Sideview conventions that allow the expressway data to be pulled into Sideview's commercial app Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics.

In practical terms that means you and your team can investigate and chart your way through your expressway data in a sophisticated user interface, covering a wider range of functionality, but without having to learn the Splunk search language, aka SPL.

module.name: HTML
module.param:
The Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app is not currently installed here, but you can learn more about it on our website. It is available as a free trial and can be set up quite easily following these steps.

No matter your situation, hopefully you are talking to us and you can always reach us at info@sideviewapps.com.

module.name: HTML
module.param: (hit Escape to close, or click the little X at the top right here).

module.name: HTML
module.param: Welcome to Sideview's Supporting App for Expressway CDR $version$

module.name: Search
module.param: | rest /servicesNS/-/-/configs/conf-checklist splunk_server=local | rename eai:appName as app search as searchToRun | search app="$sideview.currentApp$" | fields app title searchToRun drilldown | search title="critical_*" | eval drilldownLabel=if(isnull(drilldown),"see health check results","run troubleshooting search") | eval drilldown=if(isnull(drilldown),searchToRun,drilldown)

module.name: HTML
module.param: One or more critical health checks have failed and should be addressed as soon as possible:

module.name: HTML
module.param: If you need more detail or help fixing these issues, contact us at support@sideviewapps.com

module.name: Search
module.param:
`custom_index` ( sourcetype="cisco_expressway_syslog") | spath input=details | foreach Call.Legs{}.Leg.* [eval '<<FIELD>>'=if('<<FIELD>>'=="", "x", '<<FIELD>>') | eval <<MATCHSTR>>=mvappend('<<MATCHSTR>>', '<<FIELD>>')] | fields - Call.Legs{}.Leg.* | rename EncryptionType as encryption Call.Licensed as "licensed" Call.LicensedAsTraversal as "licensed_as_traversal" | eval licensed=if(licensed=="True","licensed",null()) | eval licensed_as_traversal=if(licensed_as_traversal=="True","licensed_as_traversal",null()) | eval encryption = mvfilter(encryption!="None") | eval {licensed}_call_id = call_id | eval {licensed_as_traversal}_call_id = call_id | fields call_id *_call_id origDeviceName destDeviceName callingPartyNumber calledPartyNumber encryption | eval encrypted_call_id = if(isnotnull(encryption),call_id,null())
| stats dc(call_id) as calls dc(encrypted_call_id) as encrypted_calls values(encryption) as encryption_types dc(origDeviceName) as "originating devices" dc(destDeviceName) as "destination devices" dc(callingPartyNumber) as partyNumbers | eval encryption_types=mvjoin(encryption_types,", ")

module.name: HTML
module.param: WARNING: The Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app is not currently installed. Drilldowns on this page will only include links to the generic Splunk Search UI using raw SPL.

module.name: HTML
module.param:
WARNING: The Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app is installed but the Expressway data_type has not been enabled yet.
TO FIX: Navigate to the Enable/disable data types page in the Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app, and enable the Expressway data_type there.

In the meantime drilldowns on this page will only include links to the generic Splunk Search UI using raw SPL.

module.name: Link
module.param: Investigate these calls in the Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` ( sourcetype="cisco_expressway_syslog") | eval endTime=_time + duration | stats min(_time) as _time max(endTime) as end by call_id | where isnotnull(end) | eval duration=end-_time ``` inline version of the Cisco CDR apps get_concurrency macro, the simpler version with no split-by ``` | eval increment = if(duration>0,mvappend("1","-1"),"0") | mvexpand increment | eval _time = if(increment==1, _time, _time + duration) | sort 0 + _time | streamstats sum(increment) as post_concurrency | eval concurrency = if(increment==-1, post_concurrency+1, post_concurrency) ``` inline version of the Cisco CDR apps timechart_for_concurrency macro, the simpler version with no split-by``` | timechart fixedrange=false bins=400 max(concurrency) as max_concurrency last(post_concurrency) as last_concurrency | filldown last_concurrency* | eval concurrency=coalesce(max_concurrency,last_concurrency) | fields - last_concurrency max_concurrency

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` ( sourcetype="cisco_expressway_syslog") callingPartyNumber=* NOT callingPartyNumber="" | chart dc(call_id) as calls over callingPartyNumber | sort 0 calls desc | head 20

module.name: HTML
module.param: chart calls with $chart.xValue$ over time (cisco_cdr not installed)

module.name: HTML
module.param: investigate calls with $chart.xValue$ (cisco_cdr is installed but expressway data is disabled)

module.name: HTML
module.param: chart calls with $chart.xValue$ over time (cisco_cdr is installed but expressway data is disabled)

module.name: Link
module.param: view raw CSV events in Splunk

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` ( sourcetype="cisco_expressway_syslog") calledPartyNumber=* NOT calledPartyNumber="" | chart dc(call_id) over hour_of_day by day_of_week

module.name: HTML
module.param: chart top callingPartyNames for $chart.searchTerms$ (cisco_cdr not installed)

module.name: HTML
module.param: investigate calls with $chart.searchTerms$ (cisco_cdr is installed but expressway data is disabled)

module.name: HTML
module.param: chart top callingPartyNames for $chart.searchTerms$ (cisco_cdr is installed but expressway data is disabled)

module.name: Link
module.param: view raw CSV events in Splunk
